top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 3288 clips (96.3%) — sell to brands
top_strategic.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 3288 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_strategic.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Idea Lab as a White-Label SaaS
top_strategic.evidence: Licensing the Idea Lab itself to adjacent sports (tennis, padel, pickleball leagues, badminton, bowling) and non-sports (fashion retail, restaurant serviceware, retail shelf analytics). A paddle tennis facility buys 'Paddle DaaS' with the same Idea Lab framework. An athletic apparel retailer buys 'Fit DaaS' analyzing video of products in the wild. We become the underlying research OS, not the pickleball company. Each vertical's Idea Lab feeds insights into a shared model — massive network effects.

top_strategic.title: Crowdsourced Coaching Bounty Board
top_strategic.evidence: Reverse the supply chain: post clips anonymously from the Courtana corpus on a bounty board. Coaches bid to analyze them and provide feedback. Best feedback is algorithmically scored by the Idea Lab and gets paid. Coaches build portfolios. The Courtana player eventually gets the feedback for free or low-cost (paid by brand sponsorships). Coaches compete to be better, making the product better. Brands get to sponsor coaching feedback. Creates a gig-economy moat and crowdsources improvement.

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 164 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(913x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Tmf-JOOLA(244x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(205x), Santinofuntila-JOOLA(161x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.91, volley=3.58, dink=2.98, drive=2.89, serve=2.18, unknown=1.2, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.98 (precision=0.99, recall=0.98)
top_data.evidence: Across 460 verified clips: 1821 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.985.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Worst predicted badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.955), hustler (F1=0.919)
top_data.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.955), hustler (F1=0.919). These need prompt improvement.
top_data.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 3385 clips with signatures avg 3.0 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 3385 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 3.0 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

all_findings.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
all_findings.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (446/5638 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue), Onix (3/38 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=5192, Nike=566, PICKLEBALL=171, HEAD=101, Reolink=98.
all_findings.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.41 vs 4.74) + watchability (6.79 vs 2.78)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.41, watchability=6.79. Bottom 100: quality=4.74, watchability=2.78. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.7 shots. Long rallies (15+): 1758 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 58 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 338/460 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 73% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 210.0x more likely in top clips (21.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 21.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.action: Use shot type as player skill segmentation signal

all_findings.title: Coaching Franchise API
all_findings.evidence: Don't sell coaching TO facilities — become the hidden coaching engine INSIDE 50 existing coaching apps and platforms (Think Tennis.com, EDGE, etc.). Our Idea Lab powers their AI coach. They white-label it, we take 15-30% of their subscription revenue. Every coach that uses our API makes our model better. Creates a defensible moat: coaching platforms get locked in, we own the data network effect, brands get exposure inside coaching workflows they can't reach otherwise.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: partnership_flip play

all_findings.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 64 players with 5+ clips
all_findings.evidence: 64 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(540), Chintan(186), Tmf(105), Ironvarr(98), Irenefuntila(89)
all_findings.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

product_concept.name: BrandDetect Training Studio
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 5 missed badge types (e.g., 'shoe logos on court', 'racket brand markers') with side-by-side comparison: current detection vs. positive training examples. User can approve/reject detections to auto-retrain the model.
product_concept.why_powerful: Turns detection gaps into crowdsourced training data, closing the exact 5 badge blind spots while improving sponsorship inventory accuracy.

product_concept.name: True Share Calculator
product_concept.first_screen: Input screen for venue/net type selection, showing a pre-scan of detected brands (7 flagged as potentially inflated). Displays raw brand counts vs. corrected 'true share-of-court %' after venue bias removal, with downloadable sponsorship audit report.
product_concept.why_powerful: Enables sponsorship sales teams to pitch corrected metrics to brands, proving actual court presence vs. inflated venue-bias numbers and unlocking 4 unbranded equipment categories.

product_concept.name: SponsorshipGap Intelligence
product_concept.first_screen: Map view of 3288 clips color-coded by sponsorship saturation; 96.3% show whitespace. Clicking a clip zone displays true brand share (corrected for venue bias), unbranded equipment categories, and a 'sell this moment' button generating a sponsor pitch deck with corrected metrics.
product_concept.why_powerful: Packages massive unsponsored inventory with bias-corrected proof-of-reach, turning detection gaps into a searchable sponsorship marketplace for brands targeting specific equipment categories.
